key_insight: A Lean proof agent can improve its own proof workflow by self-modifying under trusted Lean verification while the benchmark difficulty coevolves with its capability.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical path to more robust theorem-proving systems: evolve prompts, tools, and proof context together, but keep success tied to machine-checked Lean proofs.
method: Small trusted runtime; fully mutable workspace for workflow, prompts, and tools.
Champion agent revises the task distribution with a mastery-throttled curriculum that adds harder proofs only after mastering current ones.
Single-anchor recalibration re-scores the champion on the updated benchmark to keep comparisons fair as tasks get harder.
evidence_signal: Over 15 active generations, the best coevolving agent reached 45.1% solve rate on held-out miniF2F, compared with 12.7% for the seed and 32.0% for the best fixed-benchmark baseline.
watch_item: Because the benchmark itself changes during evolution, the approach depends on careful recalibration and trusted verification to avoid misleading score gains or benchmark drift.
paper_title: Self-Modifying Lean Proof Agents with Verifier-Grounded Benchmark Coevolution
